info.title: WorldAtlas API
info.description: REST API for WorldAtlas travel platform

get.description: Root health check - returns basic status.

Detailed health check with dependency verification.

Returns:
    dict: Status information including database and services

post.description:
Register a new user account.

Creates a new user with the provided information, hashes the password,
generates email verification token, sends verification email,
and returns JWT access + refresh tokens for immediate authentication.

The refresh token is set as an httpOnly cookie for security.

Args:
    request: Registration data (fullname, email, password, country)
    response: FastAPI response object for setting cookies
    db: Database session

Returns:
    TokenResponse: JWT access token and user information

Raises:
    HTTPException: 409 if email already exists
    HTTPException: 400 if database constraint violation occurs

Authenticate user and return JWT tokens.

Validates user credentials, issues access + refresh tokens,
and sets refresh token as httpOnly cookie.

Args:
    request: Login credentials (email, password)
    response: FastAPI response object for setting cookies
    db: Database session

Returns:
    TokenResponse: JWT access token and user information

Raises:
    HTTPException: 401 if credentials are invalid
    HTTPException: 403 if account is inactive

Refresh access token using refresh token.

Validates the refresh token, rotates it (issues new refresh token),
and returns a new access token. The old refresh token is revoked.

The refresh token can be provided either:
- In the request body as JSON: {"refresh_token": "..."}
- As an httpOnly cookie (automatically sent by browser)

Args:
    response: FastAPI response object for setting new cookie
    refresh_token: Refresh token from request body (optional)
    db: Database session

Returns:
    dict: New access token and token type

Raises:
    HTTPException: 401 if refresh token is invalid, expired, or revoked

Logout user by revoking refresh token.

Revokes the current refresh token and clears the httpOnly cookie.
This logs out the user from the current device only.

Args:
    response: FastAPI response object for clearing cookie
    refresh_token: Refresh token from request body (optional)
    db: Database session

Returns:
    204 No Content

Raises:
    HTTPException: 401 if refresh token is invalid

Logout user from all devices.

Revokes all refresh tokens for the current user,
effectively logging them out from all devices.

Requires authentication via access token.

Args:
    response: FastAPI response object for clearing cookie
    current_user: Authenticated user from JWT token
    db: Database session

Returns:
    204 No Content

get.description:
Verify user email address.

Validates the email verification token and marks the user's
email as verified. The token can only be used once.

Args:
    token: Email verification token from email link
    db: Database session

Returns:
    dict: Success message

Raises:
    HTTPException: 400 if token is invalid, expired, or already used

post.description:
Request password reset.

Generates a password reset token and sends it to the user's email.
Always returns success even if email doesn't exist (security best practice).

Args:
    email: User's email address
    db: Database session

Returns:
    dict: Success message

Reset user password.

Validates the password reset token and updates the user's password.
The token can only be used once.

Args:
    token: Password reset token from email link
    new_password: New password (min 8 characters)
    db: Database session

Returns:
    dict: Success message

Raises:
    HTTPException: 400 if token is invalid, expired, or already used
    HTTPException: 400 if password is too short

get.description:
Get current authenticated user profile.

Returns the profile information of the currently authenticated user.
Requires a valid JWT access token in the Authorization header.

Args:
    current_user: Authenticated user from JWT token

Returns:
    UserResponse: User profile information

Redirect to Google OAuth2 consent screen.

Generates a random state parameter for CSRF protection and PKCE
code challenge for enhanced security, then redirects the user to
Google's OAuth2 authorization page.

Returns:
    RedirectResponse: Redirect to Google OAuth2 consent screen

Example:
    GET /auth/google
    → Redirects to https://accounts.google.com/o/oauth2/v2/auth?...

Handle Google OAuth2 callback.

Validates the state parameter, exchanges the authorization code for tokens,
fetches user profile from Google, creates or links the user account,
and returns JWT access + refresh tokens.

Args:
    code: Authorization code from Google
    state: State parameter for CSRF validation
    response: FastAPI response object for setting cookies
    db: Database session

Returns:
    TokenResponse: JWT access token and user information

Raises:
    HTTPException: 400 if state is invalid or token exchange fails
    HTTPException: 400 if user info fetch fails

Example:
    GET /auth/google/callback?code=4/xxx&state=yyy
    → Returns {access_token, token_type, user}

Redirect to Facebook OAuth2 consent screen.

Generates a random state parameter for CSRF protection,
then redirects the user to Facebook's OAuth2 authorization page.

Returns:
    RedirectResponse: Redirect to Facebook OAuth2 consent screen

Example:
    GET /auth/facebook
    → Redirects to https://www.facebook.com/v18.0/dialog/oauth?...

Handle Facebook OAuth2 callback.

Validates the state parameter, exchanges the authorization code for tokens,
fetches user profile from Facebook, creates or links the user account,
and returns JWT access + refresh tokens.

Args:
    code: Authorization code from Facebook
    state: State parameter for CSRF validation
    response: FastAPI response object for setting cookies
    db: Database session

Returns:
    TokenResponse: JWT access token and user information

Raises:
    HTTPException: 400 if state is invalid or token exchange fails
    HTTPException: 400 if user info fetch fails

Example:
    GET /auth/facebook/callback?code=xxx&state=yyy
    → Returns {access_token, token_type, user}

Redirect to Google OAuth2 consent screen.

Generates a random state parameter for CSRF protection and PKCE
code challenge for enhanced security, then redirects the user to
Google's OAuth2 authorization page.

Returns:
    RedirectResponse: Redirect to Google OAuth2 consent screen

Example:
    GET /auth/google
    → Redirects to https://accounts.google.com/o/oauth2/v2/auth?...

Handle Google OAuth2 callback.

Validates the state parameter, exchanges the authorization code for tokens,
fetches user profile from Google, creates or links the user account,
and returns JWT access + refresh tokens.

Args:
    code: Authorization code from Google
    state: State parameter for CSRF validation
    response: FastAPI response object for setting cookies
    db: Database session

Returns:
    TokenResponse: JWT access token and user information

Raises:
    HTTPException: 400 if state is invalid or token exchange fails
    HTTPException: 400 if user info fetch fails

Example:
    GET /auth/google/callback?code=4/xxx&state=yyy
    → Returns {access_token, token_type, user}

Redirect to Facebook OAuth2 consent screen.

Generates a random state parameter for CSRF protection,
then redirects the user to Facebook's OAuth2 authorization page.

Returns:
    RedirectResponse: Redirect to Facebook OAuth2 consent screen

Example:
    GET /auth/facebook
    → Redirects to https://www.facebook.com/v18.0/dialog/oauth?...

Handle Facebook OAuth2 callback.

Validates the state parameter, exchanges the authorization code for tokens,
fetches user profile from Facebook, creates or links the user account,
and returns JWT access + refresh tokens.

Args:
    code: Authorization code from Facebook
    state: State parameter for CSRF validation
    response: FastAPI response object for setting cookies
    db: Database session

Returns:
    TokenResponse: JWT access token and user information

Raises:
    HTTPException: 400 if state is invalid or token exchange fails
    HTTPException: 400 if user info fetch fails

Example:
    GET /auth/facebook/callback?code=xxx&state=yyy
    → Returns {access_token, token_type, user}

Get current user profile.

Returns the authenticated user's profile information including
personal details, role, subscription status, and avatar.

Args:
    current_user: Authenticated user from JWT token

Returns:
    UserResponse: User profile data

put.description:
Update current user profile.

Allows updating fullname, country, and preferred_language.
Only provided fields will be updated.

Args:
    updates: Fields to update (all optional)
    current_user: Authenticated user from JWT token
    db: Database session

Returns:
    UserResponse: Updated user profile

delete.description:
Delete user account (soft delete).

Marks the account as deleted without removing data.
Requires password confirmation and explicit confirmation flag.
All associated data is preserved but inaccessible to the deleted user.

Args:
    data: Password confirmation and deletion reason
    current_user: Authenticated user from JWT token
    db: Database session

Returns:
    None (204 No Content)

Raises:
    HTTPException 400: If confirmation flag is not set
    HTTPException 400: If password is incorrect

post.description:
Upload user avatar image.

Uploads an avatar image to local storage or Cloudinary.
Replaces any existing avatar.

Supported formats: JPEG, PNG, WEBP
Maximum size: 5MB

Args:
    file: Avatar image file
    storage: Storage provider ("local" or "cloudinary")
    current_user: Authenticated user from JWT token
    db: Database session

Returns:
    UserResponse: Updated user profile with new avatar URL

put.description:
Update user avatar URL.

Sets or updates the user's avatar image URL.

Args:
    avatar_data: New avatar URL
    current_user: Authenticated user from JWT token
    db: Database session

Returns:
    UserResponse: Updated user profile with new avatar

delete.description:
Remove user avatar.

Deletes the user's avatar file from storage and sets avatar_url to None.

Args:
    current_user: Authenticated user from JWT token
    db: Database session

Returns:
    None (204 No Content)

get.description:
Get user dashboard statistics.

Returns counts of user's favorites, albums, and reviews along with
the user profile information.

Args:
    current_user: Authenticated user from JWT token
    db: Database session

Returns:
    UserDashboardResponse: User statistics and profile

post.description:
Change user password.

Requires current password verification and new password confirmation.
All active sessions remain valid (use logout to invalidate tokens).

Args:
    data: Current password, new password, and confirmation
    current_user: Authenticated user from JWT token
    db: Database session

Returns:
    None (204 No Content)

Raises:
    HTTPException 400: If current password is incorrect or passwords don't match
    HTTPException 400: If new password doesn't meet requirements

get.description:
List current user's revenue periods (owner only).

Returns all revenue periods for the authenticated owner.
Requires the user to be a business owner.

- **period_type**: Optional filter (monthly, quarterly, yearly)
- **payment_status**: Optional filter (pending, processing, paid, failed)

schema.title: Response Get My Revenues Api V1 Users Me Revenues Get

get.description:
Get revenue summary for current user (owner only).

Returns aggregated revenue statistics across all periods
including total gross/net revenue, pending amounts, and
transaction counts.

List user's favorite destinations with pagination.

Returns a paginated list of destinations that the authenticated user
has marked as favorites, ordered by most recently added.

Args:
    page: Page number (default: 1)
    per_page: Items per page (default: 20, max: 100)
    current_user: Authenticated user from JWT token
    db: Database session

Returns:
    PaginatedResponse[DestinationResponse]: Paginated list of favorite destinations

post.description:
Toggle a destination as favorite for the authenticated user.

If the destination is already in favorites, it will be removed (toggle off).
If it is not, it will be added (toggle on).

Args:
    destination_id: ID of the destination to favorite/unfavorite
    response: FastAPI Response object for setting status code
    current_user: Authenticated user from JWT token
    db: Database session

Returns:
    dict: Success message

Raises:
    HTTPException: 404 if destination not found

delete.description:
Remove a destination from user's favorites.

Removes the specified destination from the authenticated user's favorites.
Returns 404 Not Found if the favorite doesn't exist.

Args:
    destination_id: ID of the destination to unfavorite
    current_user: Authenticated user from JWT token
    db: Database session

Returns:
    None (204 No Content)

Raises:
    HTTPException: 404 if favorite not found

get.description:
List current user's albums.

Requires authentication.

schema.title: Response Get User Albums Api V1 Albums Get

post.description:
Create a new album.

Requires authentication.

get.description:
Get album detail with places and images.

Requires authentication. Users can view their own albums or public albums.

put.description:
Update album metadata.

Requires authentication. Only album owner can update.

delete.description:
Delete album.

Requires authentication. Only album owner can delete.

post.description:
Add a place to album.

Requires authentication. Only album owner can add places.

delete.description:
Remove place from album.

Requires authentication. Only album owner can remove places.

post.description:
Upload image to album.

Requires authentication. Only album owner can upload images.

delete.description:
Delete image from album.

Requires authentication. Only album owner can delete images.

post.summary: Generate a story from album photos
post.description: Generate an immersive travel story based on the album photos. Requires Premium or Family plan.
Generate a captivating description and title suggestion for the album. Requires Premium or Family plan.

get.description: Get paginated reviews for a specific destination.
Get review statistics (average rating, count, distribution) for a destination.

post.description: Create a new review for a destination. Users can only review a destination once.

get.description: List all reviews created by the current user.

schema.title: Response Get My Reviews Api V1 Reviews Me Get

get.description: Get a specific review by its ID.

put.description: Update a review created by the current user.

delete.description: Delete a review created by the current user.

get.description: Get all reviews with optional filtering. Admin only.

schema.title: Response List All Reviews Api V1 Reviews Admin All Get

delete.description: Delete any review by ID. Admin only.

get.description: List all countries.

schema.title: Response List Countries Api V1 Countries Get

get.description: List regions for a specific country.

schema.title: Response List Regions By Country Api V1 Regions  Country Code  Get

get.description:
Get personalized recommendations for authenticated user.

Requires authentication. Recommendations are based on user's visit history,
category affinity, and budget preferences.

schema.title: Response Get User Recommendations Endpoint Api V1 Recommendations Get

get.description:
Get trending places (public).

Trending places are based on analytics data including view counts,
bookings, and orders over the last 7 days.

schema.title: Response Get Trending Places Endpoint Api V1 Recommendations Trending Get

get.description:
Get similar places to a given place (public).

Similarity is based on category, budget range, season, and location proximity.

schema.title: Response Get Similar Places Endpoint Api V1 Recommendations Places  Slug  Similar Get

get.description:
List all place claims registered by the authenticated owner.

Supports pagination with page and per_page query parameters.

**Authorization:** Requires owner or admin role.

**Returns:**
- List of place claims owned by the current user
- Paginated results (default: 20 per page, max: 100)

schema.title: Response List Claims Api V1 Owner Claims Get

post.description:
Register a new place claim for the authenticated owner.

**Authorization:** Requires owner or admin role.

**Validation:**
- `place_name` is required (min 1 character, max 255)
- `category` is required (defaults to "other")
- All other fields are optional

**Returns:**
- The created place claim with status "pending"
- HTTP 201 Created on success

get.description:
Get detailed information about a specific place claim.

**Authorization:** Requires owner or admin role.

**Access Control:**
- Owners can only view their own claims
- Admins can view any claim

**Returns:**
- Place claim details
- HTTP 404 if claim doesn't exist
- HTTP 403 if owner tries to access another owner's claim

put.description:
Update an existing place claim.

**Authorization:** Requires owner or admin role.

**Access Control:**
- Owners can only update their own claims
- Admins can update any claim

**Validation:**
- All fields are optional in the update
- Only provided fields will be updated
- Cannot change owner_id or status (use admin endpoints for status changes)

**Returns:**
- Updated place claim
- HTTP 404 if claim doesn't exist
- HTTP 403 if owner tries to update another owner's claim

get.description:
Get platform-wide statistics for admin dashboard.

**Authorization:** Requires admin role.

**Returns:**
- Total counts for users, destinations, reviews, claims, favorites, and albums
- Count of pending place claims
- HTTP 403 if user is not admin

List all destinations with pagination (admin only).

**Authorization:** Requires admin role.

**Query Parameters:**
- `page`: Page number (default: 1)
- `per_page`: Items per page (default: 20, max: 100)

**Returns:**
- Paginated list of all destinations with relationships
- HTTP 403 if user is not admin

schema.title: Response List All Destinations Api V1 Admin Destinations Get

put.description:
Update any field of a destination (admin only).

**Authorization:** Requires admin role.

**Validation:**
- All fields are optional
- Only provided fields will be updated
- `safety_level` must be between 1 and 5

**Returns:**
- Updated destination with all relationships
- HTTP 404 if destination doesn't exist
- HTTP 403 if user is not admin

get.description:
List pending place claims with pagination (admin only).

**Authorization:** Requires admin role.

**Query Parameters:**
- `page`: Page number (default: 1)
- `per_page`: Items per page (default: 20, max: 100)

**Returns:**
- Paginated list of place claims with status "pending"
- HTTP 403 if user is not admin

schema.title: Response List Pending Claims Api V1 Admin Claims Get

put.description:
Approve or reject a place claim by updating its status (admin only).

**Authorization:** Requires admin role.

**Validation:**
- `status` must be one of: "pending", "verified", "rejected"
- `admin_notes` is optional

**Returns:**
- Updated place claim with new status
- HTTP 404 if claim doesn't exist
- HTTP 403 if user is not admin

get.description: Get all owner revenues with optional filters for period type, payment status, and date range. Admin only.

schema.title: Response List Owner Revenues Api V1 Admin Analytics Owner Revenues Get

get.description: Get global revenue statistics for admin dashboard. Includes totals, payment status breakdown, and growth metrics.
Get detailed revenue summary for a specific owner including totals across all periods and pending amounts.
Get all revenue periods for a specific owner.

schema.title: Response List Owner Revenue Periods Api V1 Admin Analytics Owner Revenues  Owner Id  Periods Get

put.description: Update payment status for a specific revenue record. Use this to mark revenues as paid or processing.

get.description: Export filtered owner revenues to CSV format. Supports same filters as the list endpoint. Admin only.

post.description:
Create a Stripe checkout session for subscription.

Creates a Stripe checkout session for the specified plan and returns
the checkout URL where the user can complete payment.

Args:
    plan_id: The subscription plan ID (e.g., "premium", "pro")
    current_user: The authenticated user
    db: Database session

Returns:
    CheckoutSessionResponse: Checkout URL and session ID

Raises:
    HTTPException: 503 if Stripe is not configured
    HTTPException: 400 if plan_id is invalid

Handle Stripe webhook events.

Processes Stripe webhook events, particularly checkout.session.completed
to update user subscription status after successful payment.

Args:
    request: FastAPI request containing webhook payload
    db: Database session

Returns:
    WebhookResponse: Success status

Raises:
    HTTPException: 400 if payload or signature is invalid

get.description:
List current user's bookings with optional status filter.

Requires authentication.

post.description:
Create a new booking with Stripe payment.

Requires authentication.

get.description:
Get booking detail.

Requires authentication. Users can only view their own bookings.

post.description:
Confirm a booking after payment verification.

Requires authentication. Users can only confirm their own bookings.

Cancel booking and initiate refund if paid.

Requires authentication. Users can only cancel their own bookings.

get.description:
List bookings for a place (owner/admin only).

Requires owner or admin authentication.

List current user's orders with optional status filter.

Requires authentication.

post.description:
Create a new order with Stripe payment.

Requires authentication.

get.description:
Get order detail with items.

Requires authentication. Users can only view their own orders.

post.description:
Confirm an order after payment verification.

Requires authentication. Users can only confirm their own orders.

Cancel order and initiate refund if paid.

Requires authentication. Users can only cancel their own orders.

get.description: Get current user's cart with all items and product details.

delete.description: Clear all items from the cart.

post.description: Add a product to the cart. If product already exists, quantity is updated.

put.description: Update quantity of a cart item.

delete.description: Remove an item from the cart.

post.description:
Convert cart to order items for checkout.
Returns order items and clears the cart.

schema.title: Response Checkout Cart Api V1 Cart Checkout Post

get.description: Get cart summary (item count and subtotal) without full details.

schema.title: Response Get Cart Summary Endpoint Api V1 Cart Summary Get

get.description: List all saved delivery addresses for the user.

schema.title: Response List Addresses Api V1 Delivery Addresses Get

post.description: Create a new delivery address with automatic geocoding.

get.description: Get a specific delivery address.

put.description: Update a delivery address (with re-geocoding if address changed).

get.description: Get user's default delivery address.

schema.title: Response Get Default Api V1 Delivery Addresses Default Get

post.description:
Estimate delivery fee for an address.
Calculates distance, zone pricing, and applicable discounts.

Create a delivery request for an order.
Generates tracking number and QR code.

get.description: List user's delivery requests with optional status filter.
Get delivery details with tracking events and address.

Public tracking endpoint - no authentication required.
Anyone with tracking number can check delivery status.

put.description:
Update delivery status (admin/courier only).
Triggers tracking event and notifications.

post.description:
Update courier location (courier app only).
Auto-detects 'nearby' status when within 500m of destination.

get.description: Get user's delivery preferences.
List all active delivery zones.

schema.title: Response List Zones Api V1 Delivery Zones Get

post.description: Create a new delivery zone (admin only).

get.description:
Get place analytics for owner (owner/admin only).

Requires owner or admin authentication.

schema.title: Response Get Place Analytics Endpoint Api V1 Analytics Places  Slug  Get

get.description:
Get global platform analytics (admin only).

Requires admin authentication.

schema.title: Response Get Global Analytics Endpoint Api V1 Analytics Global Get

get.description:
Get current user's visit history.

Requires authentication.

post.description:
Record a place visit (authenticated or anonymous).

Can be called by authenticated or anonymous users.

get.description:
List all advertisements with pagination.

This endpoint is public and returns a paginated list of advertisements
that the frontend can display. Only approved advertisements are shown
by default (unless a status filter is provided by admin).

- **page**: Page number (default: 1)
- **per_page**: Items per page (default: 10, max: 100)
- **status_filter**: Optional status filter (default: only ACTIVE)

List current user's advertisements.

Requires authentication.

schema.title: Response Get User Advertisements Api V1 Advertisements Get

post.description:
Create a new advertisement.

Requires authentication.

schema.title: Response Create New Advertisement Api V1 Advertisements Post

get.description:
Get advertisement detail.

Requires authentication. Users can only view their own advertisements.

schema.title: Response Get Advertisement Detail Api V1 Advertisements  Ad Id  Get

put.description:
Update advertisement (draft only).

Requires authentication. Only draft advertisements can be updated.

schema.title: Response Update Advertisement Api V1 Advertisements  Ad Id  Put

post.description:
Submit advertisement for review.

Requires authentication.

schema.title: Response Submit Advertisement Endpoint Api V1 Advertisements  Ad Id  Submit Post

post.description:
Record impression (internal/CDN).

This endpoint is typically called by CDN or internal systems.

schema.title: Response Record Ad Impression Endpoint Api V1 Advertisements  Ad Id  Impression Post

post.description:
Record click (internal).

This endpoint is typically called by internal systems.

schema.title: Response Record Ad Click Endpoint Api V1 Advertisements  Ad Id  Click Post

get.description:
List all ads for admin review.

Requires admin authentication.

schema.title: Response Get All Advertisements For Admin Api V1 Advertisements Admin Ads Get

put.description:
Approve or reject advertisement (admin only).

Requires admin authentication.

schema.title: Response Admin Review Advertisement Endpoint Api V1 Advertisements Admin Ads  Ad Id  Review Put

get.description:
List all active partners.

This endpoint is public and returns a list of active partners.

schema.title: Response Get Partners Api V1 Advertisements Partners Get

post.description:
Create a new partner (admin only).

Requires admin authentication.

get.description:
Get partner details by ID.

This endpoint is public.

put.description:
Update partner details (admin only).

Requires admin authentication.

delete.description:
Soft delete a partner (admin only).

Requires admin authentication. Sets is_active to False.

get.description:
Full-text search for destinations.

Searches across destination name, short_description, and city.
Optionally filters by category.

Args:
    q: Search query string
    category: Optional category filter
    db: Database session

Returns:
    List of matching destinations

schema.title: Response Search Destinations Api V1 Get

get.description:
List places with filtering, search, sorting and pagination.

Returns a paginated list of places with optional filters for category,
city, budget range, season, and status. Only published places are shown
to unauthenticated users.

Query Parameters:
    - category: Place category (museum, park, hotel, restaurant, vr_room)
    - city: City name (case-insensitive partial match)
    - budget_range: Budget range (budget, moderate, expensive, luxury)
    - season: Best season to visit (spring, summer, autumn, winter, year_round)
    - status: Publication status (draft, published, archived)
    - is_featured: Filter featured places
    - query: Search text (searches in name and description)
    - sort_by: Sort criteria (popular, rating, recent)
    - page: Page number (default: 1)
    - page_size: Items per page (default: 20, max: 100)

Returns:
    PaginatedResponse with place list items

Example:
    GET /places?category=museum&city=Lomé&page=1&page_size=20
    GET /places?query=musée&sort_by=rating

post.description:
Create a new place (owner/admin only).

Creates a new place with the provided information. The slug is automatically
generated from the place name and ensured to be unique.

Requires authentication with owner or admin role.

Args:
    place_data: Place creation data
    current_user: Authenticated user (owner or admin)
    db: Database session

Returns:
    PlaceResponse: Created place with all details

Raises:
    HTTPException: 400 if validation fails
    HTTPException: 403 if user is not owner/admin

Example:
    POST /places
    {
        "name": "Musée National du Togo",
        "category": "museum",
        "description": "...",
        "city": "Lomé",
        "status": "draft"
    }

get.description:
Search places using full-text and/or geospatial search.

Supports three search modes:
1. Text search: Search by name, description, city, address
2. Geospatial search: Find places within radius of coordinates
3. Combined: Text search + geospatial filtering

Query Parameters:
    - query: Text search query (searches name, description, city, address)
    - lat: Latitude for geospatial search
    - lon: Longitude for geospatial search
    - radius: Radius in kilometers (required if lat/lon provided)
    - limit: Maximum number of results (default: 20, max: 100)

Returns:
    List of matching places

Examples:
    GET /places/search?query=museum
    GET /places/search?lat=6.1319&lon=1.2228&radius=10
    GET /places/search?query=restaurant&lat=6.1319&lon=1.2228&radius=5

schema.title: Response Search Places Endpoint Api V1 Places Search Get

get.description:
Find places within radius of coordinates.

Returns places sorted by distance from the provided coordinates.
Only published places are returned.

Query Parameters:
    - lat: Latitude (required)
    - lon: Longitude (required)
    - radius: Radius in kilometers (default: 10, max: 1000)
    - limit: Maximum number of results (default: 20, max: 100)

Returns:
    List of nearby places sorted by distance

Example:
    GET /places/nearby?lat=6.1319&lon=1.2228&radius=10

schema.title: Response Get Nearby Places Api V1 Places Nearby Get

get.description:
Get unique list of cities with published places.

Returns a sorted list of unique city names where published places exist.
Useful for search filters in the frontend.

Query Parameters:
    - None

Returns:
    List[str]: Sorted list of unique city names (e.g., ["Lomé", "Kpalimé", "Kara"])

Example:
    GET /places/cities
    Response: ["Kara", "Kpalimé", "Lomé"]

schema.title: Response Get Unique Cities Api V1 Places Cities Get

get.description:
Get place details by slug.

Returns complete place information including images, products, and VR sessions.
Increments the view count automatically.

Args:
    slug: Place slug (URL-friendly identifier)
    db: Database session

Returns:
    PlaceResponse: Complete place details with relationships

Raises:
    HTTPException: 404 if place not found

Example:
    GET /places/musee-national-togo

put.description:
Update place (owner/admin only).

Updates place information. Only the place owner or admin can update.
If the name is changed, the slug is automatically regenerated.

Requires authentication with owner or admin role.

Args:
    slug: Place slug
    place_data: Place update data (all fields optional)
    current_user: Authenticated user (owner or admin)
    db: Database session

Returns:
    PlaceResponse: Updated place details

Raises:
    HTTPException: 404 if place not found
    HTTPException: 403 if user is not the owner or admin

Example:
    PUT /places/musee-national-togo
    {
        "description": "Updated description",
        "status": "published"
    }

delete.description:
Soft delete place (owner/admin only).

Marks the place as deleted by setting the deleted_at timestamp.
The place is not physically removed from the database.

Requires authentication with owner or admin role.

Args:
    slug: Place slug
    current_user: Authenticated user (owner or admin)
    db: Database session

Returns:
    204 No Content

Raises:
    HTTPException: 404 if place not found
    HTTPException: 403 if user is not the owner or admin

Example:
    DELETE /places/musee-national-togo

post.description:
Upload image for place (owner/admin only).

Uploads an image to either local storage or Cloudinary.
The first image uploaded is automatically set as primary.

Supported formats: JPEG, PNG, WEBP
Maximum size: 10MB

Requires authentication with owner or admin role.

Args:
    slug: Place slug
    file: Image file to upload
    storage: Storage provider ("local" or "cloudinary")
    current_user: Authenticated user (owner or admin)
    db: Database session

Returns:
    PlaceImageUploadResponse: Uploaded image details

Raises:
    HTTPException: 404 if place not found
    HTTPException: 403 if user is not the owner or admin
    HTTPException: 400 if file validation fails

Example:
    POST /places/musee-national-togo/images?storage=cloudinary
    Content-Type: multipart/form-data
    file: <image file>

delete.description:
Delete place image (owner/admin only).

Deletes an image from the place. If the deleted image was primary,
the next image (by display_order) is automatically set as primary.

Requires authentication with owner or admin role.

Args:
    slug: Place slug
    image_id: Image UUID to delete
    current_user: Authenticated user (owner or admin)
    db: Database session

Returns:
    204 No Content

Raises:
    HTTPException: 404 if place or image not found
    HTTPException: 403 if user is not the owner or admin

Example:
    DELETE /places/musee-national-togo/images/123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000

get.description:
List all products for a place.

Returns all products associated with the specified place.
Products are returned with their primary image.

Args:
    slug: Place slug
    db: Database session

Returns:
    List[ProductListResponse]: List of products

Raises:
    HTTPException: 404 if place not found

Example:
    GET /places/musee-national-togo/products

schema.title: Response List Products Api V1 Places  Slug  Products Get

post.description:
Create a new product for a place (owner/admin only).

Creates a new product associated with the specified place.
Only the place owner or admin can create products.

Requires authentication with owner or admin role.

Args:
    slug: Place slug
    product_data: Product creation data
    current_user: Authenticated user (owner or admin)
    db: Database session

Returns:
    ProductResponse: Created product with details

Raises:
    HTTPException: 404 if place not found
    HTTPException: 403 if user is not the owner or admin
    HTTPException: 400 if validation fails

Example:
    POST /places/musee-national-togo/products
    {
        "name": "Museum T-Shirt",
        "description": "Official museum merchandise",
        "price": 15.99,
        "stock": 50,
        "is_available": true
    }

put.description:
Update a product (owner/admin only).

Updates product information. Only the place owner or admin can update.

Requires authentication with owner or admin role.

Args:
    slug: Place slug
    product_id: Product UUID
    product_data: Product update data (all fields optional)
    current_user: Authenticated user (owner or admin)
    db: Database session

Returns:
    ProductResponse: Updated product details

Raises:
    HTTPException: 404 if place or product not found
    HTTPException: 403 if user is not the owner or admin

Example:
    PUT /places/musee-national-togo/products/123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000
    {
        "price": 19.99,
        "stock": 30
    }

delete.description:
Delete a product (owner/admin only).

Permanently deletes a product from the place.
Only the place owner or admin can delete products.

Requires authentication with owner or admin role.

Args:
    slug: Place slug
    product_id: Product UUID
    current_user: Authenticated user (owner or admin)
    db: Database session

Returns:
    204 No Content

Raises:
    HTTPException: 404 if place or product not found
    HTTPException: 403 if user is not the owner or admin

Example:
    DELETE /places/musee-national-togo/products/123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000

post.description:
Upload image for product (owner/admin only).

Uploads an image to either local storage or Cloudinary.
The first image uploaded is automatically set as primary.

Supported formats: JPEG, PNG, WEBP
Maximum size: 5MB

Requires authentication with owner or admin role.

Args:
    slug: Place slug
    product_id: Product UUID
    file: Image file to upload
    storage: Storage provider ("local" or "cloudinary")
    current_user: Authenticated user (owner or admin)
    db: Database session

Returns:
    ProductImageUploadResponse: Uploaded image details

Raises:
    HTTPException: 404 if place or product not found
    HTTPException: 403 if user is not the owner or admin
    HTTPException: 400 if file validation fails

Example:
    POST /places/musee-national-togo/products/123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000/images?storage=local
    Content-Type: multipart/form-data
    file: <image file>

get.description:
List all VR sessions for a place.

Returns all VR sessions associated with the specified place.
Only active sessions are shown to regular users.

Args:
    slug: Place slug
    db: Database session

Returns:
    List[VRSessionListResponse]: List of VR sessions

Raises:
    HTTPException: 404 if place not found

Example:
    GET /places/vr-experience-lome/vr-sessions

schema.title: Response List Vr Sessions Api V1 Places  Slug  Vr Sessions Get

post.description:
Create a new VR session for a place (owner/admin only).

Creates a new VR session associated with the specified place.
Only the place owner or admin can create VR sessions.

Requires authentication with owner or admin role.

Args:
    slug: Place slug
    session_data: VR session creation data
    current_user: Authenticated user (owner or admin)
    db: Database session

Returns:
    VRSessionResponse: Created VR session with details

Raises:
    HTTPException: 404 if place not found
    HTTPException: 403 if user is not the owner or admin
    HTTPException: 400 if validation fails

Example:
    POST /places/vr-experience-lome/vr-sessions
    {
        "title": "African Safari VR Experience",
        "description": "Immersive virtual reality safari through African wildlife",
        "duration_minutes": 30,
        "price": 25.00,
        "max_participants": 4,
        "is_active": true
    }

put.description:
Update a VR session (owner/admin only).

Updates VR session information. Only the place owner or admin can update.

Requires authentication with owner or admin role.

Args:
    slug: Place slug
    session_id: VR session UUID
    session_data: VR session update data (all fields optional)
    current_user: Authenticated user (owner or admin)
    db: Database session

Returns:
    VRSessionResponse: Updated VR session details

Raises:
    HTTPException: 404 if place or VR session not found
    HTTPException: 403 if user is not the owner or admin

Example:
    PUT /places/vr-experience-lome/vr-sessions/123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000
    {
        "price": 30.00,
        "is_active": false
    }

delete.description:
Delete a VR session (owner/admin only).

Permanently deletes a VR session from the place.
Only the place owner or admin can delete VR sessions.

Requires authentication with owner or admin role.

Args:
    slug: Place slug
    session_id: VR session UUID
    current_user: Authenticated user (owner or admin)
    db: Database session

Returns:
    204 No Content

Raises:
    HTTPException: 404 if place or VR session not found
    HTTPException: 403 if user is not the owner or admin

Example:
    DELETE /places/vr-experience-lome/vr-sessions/123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000

post.description:
Book a VR session (authenticated users only).

Creates a booking for the specified VR session.
The booking is created with 'pending' status and requires payment confirmation.

Requires authentication.

Args:
    slug: Place slug
    session_id: VR session UUID
    booking_data: Booking creation data
    current_user: Authenticated user
    db: Database session

Returns:
    VRBookingResponse: Created booking with details

Raises:
    HTTPException: 404 if place or VR session not found
    HTTPException: 400 if VR session is not active
    HTTPException: 400 if number of participants exceeds maximum
    HTTPException: 409 if time slot is already booked

Example:
    POST /places/vr-experience-lome/vr-sessions/123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000/book
    {
        "booking_date": "2024-03-15T14:00:00Z",
        "time_slot": "14:00-14:30",
        "num_participants": 2
    }

get.description:
List all available subscription plans.

Returns all active subscription plans with their features and pricing.
No authentication required - public endpoint.

Returns:
    List[SubscriptionPlanResponse]: List of active subscription plans

Example:
    GET /subscriptions/plans

schema.title: Response List Subscription Plans Api V1 Subscriptions Plans Get

post.description:
Subscribe to a subscription plan (authenticated).

Creates a Stripe subscription for the authenticated user.
If the user doesn't have a Stripe customer ID, one is created automatically.

Requires authentication.

Args:
    request: Subscription request with plan_id, billing_period, and optional payment_method_id
    current_user: Authenticated user
    db: Database session

Returns:
    SubscribeResponse: Subscription details including subscription_id and status

Raises:
    HTTPException: 404 if plan not found
    HTTPException: 400 if subscription creation fails
    HTTPException: 409 if user already has an active subscription

Example:
    POST /subscriptions/subscribe
    {
        "plan_id": "123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000",
        "billing_period": "monthly",
        "payment_method_id": "pm_1234567890"
    }

Cancel current subscription (authenticated).

Cancels the user's active Stripe subscription and reverts them to the free plan.
If the user is part of a family group, they are removed from the group.

Requires authentication.

Args:
    current_user: Authenticated user
    db: Database session

Returns:
    CancelSubscriptionResponse: Confirmation message

Raises:
    HTTPException: 400 if user has no active subscription
    HTTPException: 500 if cancellation fails

Example:
    POST /subscriptions/cancel

Upgrade to a higher subscription plan (authenticated).

Upgrades the user's current subscription to a new plan with proration.
The billing period (monthly/yearly) is preserved from the current subscription.

Requires authentication and an active subscription.

Args:
    request: Upgrade request with new_plan_id
    current_user: Authenticated user
    db: Database session

Returns:
    UpgradeResponse: Updated subscription details

Raises:
    HTTPException: 400 if user has no active subscription
    HTTPException: 404 if new plan not found
    HTTPException: 400 if upgrade fails

Example:
    POST /subscriptions/upgrade
    {
        "new_plan_id": "123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000"
    }

Stripe webhook handler (no authentication, signature verification).

Processes Stripe webhook events for subscription lifecycle management.
Verifies the webhook signature to ensure the request is from Stripe.

Handles:
- invoice.paid: Update subscription expiry
- customer.subscription.deleted: Revert to free plan

No authentication required - uses Stripe signature verification instead.

Args:
    request: FastAPI request object
    stripe_signature: Stripe signature header for verification
    db: Database session

Returns:
    StripeWebhookResponse: Success confirmation

Raises:
    HTTPException: 400 if signature verification fails
    HTTPException: 500 if webhook processing fails

Example:
    POST /subscriptions/webhook
    Headers: Stripe-Signature: t=1234567890,v1=abc123...

get.description:
Get family group details (authenticated).

Returns the family group details for the authenticated user,
including all members and subscription plan information.

Requires authentication and family group membership.

Args:
    current_user: Authenticated user
    db: Database session

Returns:
    FamilyGroupResponse: Family group details with members

Raises:
    HTTPException: 404 if user is not part of a family group

Example:
    GET /subscriptions/family

post.description:
Create a family group (premium/family plan only).

Creates a new family group for the authenticated user.
Only users with a family plan can create family groups.

Requires authentication and a family subscription plan.

Args:
    request: Family group creation request with name
    current_user: Authenticated user
    db: Database session

Returns:
    FamilyGroupResponse: Created family group details

Raises:
    HTTPException: 400 if user doesn't have a family plan
    HTTPException: 409 if user already owns a family group
    HTTPException: 500 if creation fails

Example:
    POST /subscriptions/family
    {
        "name": "Smith Family"
    }

Invite member to family group (owner only).

Sends an email invitation to join the family group.
Only the family group owner can send invitations.

Requires authentication and family group ownership.

Args:
    request: Invitation request with email
    current_user: Authenticated user (must be family group owner)
    db: Database session

Returns:
    InviteFamilyMemberResponse: Confirmation message

Raises:
    HTTPException: 404 if user doesn't own a family group
    HTTPException: 403 if user is not the family group owner
    HTTPException: 400 if invitation fails (e.g., member limit reached)

Example:
    POST /subscriptions/family/invite
    {
        "email": "member@example.com"
    }

Accept family group invitation (authenticated).

Accepts a family group invitation using the token from the invitation email.
The user is added to the family group and their subscription is updated.

Requires authentication.

Args:
    request: Accept invitation request with token and group_id
    current_user: Authenticated user
    db: Database session

Returns:
    AcceptFamilyInviteResponse: Confirmation with family group details

Raises:
    HTTPException: 400 if token is invalid, expired, or already used
    HTTPException: 409 if user is already in a family group
    HTTPException: 400 if family group is full

Example:
    POST /subscriptions/family/accept
    {
        "token": "abc123...",
        "group_id": "123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000"
    }

delete.description:
Remove family member (owner only).

Removes a member from the family group and reverts them to the free plan.
Only the family group owner can remove members.
The owner cannot remove themselves.

Requires authentication and family group ownership.

Args:
    user_id: UUID of the member to remove
    current_user: Authenticated user (must be family group owner)
    db: Database session

Returns:
    RemoveFamilyMemberResponse: Confirmation message

Raises:
    HTTPException: 404 if user doesn't own a family group
    HTTPException: 403 if trying to remove the owner
    HTTPException: 404 if member not found in the group

Example:
    DELETE /subscriptions/family/members/123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000

This enum provides predefined categories, but the database column allows

but users can specify any string value for custom categories.
